title: USDA Plan No. 7156 - 2-Bedroom Farmhouse with Carport (single-story plan + perspective)
building_type: single-family house (one-story ranch with attached carport)
creator: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service (Cooperative Farm Building Plan Exchange)
rightsholder_or_source: USDA / Internet Archive (contributing institution states item is not in copyright; US Government work)
research_notes: USDA Miscellaneous Publication No. 897, 3-sheet plan set. Page n2 (cover sheet) carries the floor plan + perspective; page n3 carries a separate SITE PLAN and terrace detail. Raster page image from archive.org BookReader.
research_features: Single-story ranch with an attached CARPORT and an expansible bedroom wing; the plan shows a combination living-dining room, two bedrooms, bath, and the covered carport that can later become a building (multi-element / expansible test case). Mid-Century open-living layout with drawn dimensions. A companion SITE PLAN (house + carport + drive + terrace + plantings) appears on page n3 of the same pamphlet.
feature_highlights: attached carport (about 660 sq ft) drawn as a roofed but open rectangle crossed by an X
open combination Living Room and Dining Area with an adjoining Terrace
two bedrooms, compact Kitchen/Workroom core, and Bath strung along the rectangle
expansible plan: main part built first, bedrooms and carport deferred
drawn-in furniture and fixtures (beds, sofas, dining set, kitchen equipment)
per-room dimensions plus a graphic scale bar in feet
perspective rendering paired with the plan on the cover sheet
supported_examples: linear point-to-point dimensions in imperial units
roadmap_examples: furniture and fixture placement (beds, sofas, dining set, kitchen fixtures)

gap_features.why: the attached carport is a roofed but unenclosed covered space (and the Terrace is an open outdoor space) that must be drawable without being a fully wall-enclosed room
